015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.settings;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.net.*;
024import java.nio.charset.*;
025import java.nio.file.*;
026import java.util.*;
027import java.util.function.*;
028
029/**
030 * A specialized {@link Setting} for string values that provides convenience methods for type conversion.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * This class extends {@link Setting} with type-specific conversion methods such as {@link #asInteger()},
034 * {@link #asBoolean()}, {@link #asCharset()}, etc.
035 */
036@SuppressWarnings({
037   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
038})
039public class StringSetting extends Setting<String> {
040
041   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
042   private static final String ARG_mapper = "mapper";
043   private static final String ARG_predicate = "predicate";
044   private static final String ARG_c = "c";
045
046   /**
047    * Creates a new StringSetting from a Settings instance and a Supplier.
048    *
049    * @param settings The Settings instance that created this setting. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
050    * @param supplier The supplier that provides the string value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
051    */
052   public StringSetting(Settings settings, Supplier<String> supplier) {
053      super(settings, supplier);
054   }
055
056   /**
057    * If a value is present, applies the provided mapping function to it and returns a StringSetting describing the result.
058    *
059    * <p>
060    * This method is specifically for String-to-String mappings. For mappings to other types, use the inherited {@link #map(Function)} method.
061    *
062    * <p>
063    * The returned StringSetting maintains its own cache, independent of this supplier.
064    * Resetting the mapped supplier does not affect this supplier, and vice versa.
065    *
066    * @param mapper A mapping function to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
067    * @return A StringSetting describing the result of applying a mapping function to the value of this StringSetting, if a value is present, otherwise an empty StringSetting.
068    */
069   public StringSetting mapString(UnaryOperator<String> mapper) {
070      assertArgNotNull(ARG_mapper, mapper);
071      return new StringSetting(getSettings(), () -> {
072         String value = get();
073         return nn(value) ? mapper.apply(value) : null;
074      });
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * If a value is present, and the value matches the given predicate, returns a StringSetting describing the value, otherwise returns an empty StringSetting.
079    *
080    * <p>
081    * The returned StringSetting maintains its own cache, independent of this supplier.
082    * Resetting the filtered supplier does not affect this supplier, and vice versa.
083    *
084    * @param predicate A predicate to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
085    * @return A StringSetting describing the value of this StringSetting if a value is present and the value matches the given predicate, otherwise an empty StringSetting.
086    */
087   @Override
088   public StringSetting filter(Predicate<? super String> predicate) {
089      assertArgNotNull(ARG_predicate, predicate);
090      return new StringSetting(getSettings(), () -> {
091         String value = get();
092         return (nn(value) && predicate.test(value)) ? value : null;
093      });
094   }
095
096   /**
097    * Converts the string value to an Integer.
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * The property value is parsed using {@link Integer#valueOf(String)}. If the property is not found
101    * or cannot be parsed as an integer, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
102    *
103    * @return The property value as an Integer, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid integer.
104    */
105   public Setting<Integer> asInteger() {
106      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> Integer.valueOf(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Converts the string value to a Long.
111    *
112    * <p>
113    * The property value is parsed using {@link Long#valueOf(String)}. If the property is not found
114    * or cannot be parsed as a long, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
115    *
116    * @return The property value as a Long, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid long.
117    */
118   public Setting<Long> asLong() {
119      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> Long.valueOf(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
120   }
121
122   /**
123    * Converts the string value to a Boolean.
124    *
125    * <p>
126    * The property value is parsed using {@link Boolean#parseBoolean(String)}, which returns <c>true</c>
127    * if the value is (case-insensitive) "true", otherwise <c>false</c>. Note that this method will
128    * return <c>Optional.of(false)</c> for any non-empty value that is not "true", and
129    * {@link Optional#empty()} only if the property is not set.
130    *
131    * @return The property value as a Boolean, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found.
132    */
133   public Setting<Boolean> asBoolean() {
134      return map(Boolean::parseBoolean);
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * Converts the string value to a Double.
139    *
140    * <p>
141    * The property value is parsed using {@link Double#valueOf(String)}. If the property is not found
142    * or cannot be parsed as a double, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
143    *
144    * @return The property value as a Double, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid double.
145    */
146   public Setting<Double> asDouble() {
147      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> Double.valueOf(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
148   }
149
150   /**
151    * Converts the string value to a Float.
152    *
153    * <p>
154    * The property value is parsed using {@link Float#valueOf(String)}. If the property is not found
155    * or cannot be parsed as a float, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
156    *
157    * @return The property value as a Float, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid float.
158    */
159   public Setting<Float> asFloat() {
160      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> Float.valueOf(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
161   }
162
163   /**
164    * Converts the string value to a File.
165    *
166    * <p>
167    * The property value is converted to a {@link File} using the {@link File#File(String)} constructor.
168    * If the property is not found, returns {@link Optional#empty()}. Note that this method does not
169    * validate that the file path is valid or that the file exists.
170    *
171    * @return The property value as a File, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found.
172    */
173   public Setting<File> asFile() {
174      return map(File::new);
175   }
176
177   /**
178    * Converts the string value to a Path.
179    *
180    * <p>
181    * The property value is converted to a {@link Path} using {@link Paths#get(String, String...)}.
182    * If the property is not found or the path string is invalid, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
183    *
184    * @return The property value as a Path, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid path.
185    */
186   public Setting<Path> asPath() {
187      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> Paths.get(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
188   }
189
190   /**
191    * Converts the string value to a URI.
192    *
193    * <p>
194    * The property value is converted to a {@link URI} using {@link URI#create(String)}.
195    * If the property is not found or the URI string is invalid, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
196    *
197    * @return The property value as a URI, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid URI.
198    */
199   public Setting<URI> asURI() {
200      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> URI.create(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
201   }
202
203   /**
204    * Converts the string value to a Charset.
205    *
206    * <p>
207    * The property value is converted to a {@link Charset} using {@link Charset#forName(String)}.
208    * If the property is not found or the charset name is not supported, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
209    *
210    * @return The property value as a Charset, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid charset.
211    */
212   public Setting<Charset> asCharset() {
213      return map(v -> safeOrNull(() -> Charset.forName(v))).filter(Objects::nonNull);
214   }
215
216   /**
217    * Converts the string value to the specified type using the Settings type conversion functions.
218    *
219    * <p>
220    * The property value is converted using {@link Settings#toType(String, Class)}. If the property is not found
221    * or cannot be converted to the specified type, returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
222    *
223    * @param <T> The target type.
224    * @param c The target class. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
225    * @return The property value as the specified type, or {@link Optional#empty()} if not found or not a valid conversion.
226    */
227   public <T> Setting<T> asType(Class<T> c) {
228      assertArgNotNull(ARG_c, c);
229      return map(v -> getSettings().toType(v, c)).filter(Objects::nonNull);
230   }
231}
